title: practical english usage - Part 348
content:
The reason why I came here was to be with my family.

Reason can be followed by a clause beginning why. .. or that....

(\oi  .  ..

irw.wn of your depression?) What’s the real reason for your depression?

I need to talk to you for two reasons.

The  preposition for is used both before and after reason.

vocabulary •  571  (the) same That's the same man that/who asked me for money yesterday.       -» Before a clause, the same... that or the same... who can be used.

2 other     structures Our  birthdays are on the very same day.

Note the expression the very same (= exactly the same).

Her hair's the same colour as her mother's, (     .. . the same colour like-...) not You've got the same idea as me. (     ... my same idea.) In a comparison, we  use the same (...) as.

Give me the same again, please, (     Give me^ame-againrplease/) We   normally use the before same.

1 the    same      (as) 571 (the)   same Marylebone Road Oxford Street In street names we  normally stress the word Road, but the word before Street.

2 street     names:    stress The road out of our village goes up a steep hill.

Cars can park on both sides of our road.

Road is used for both town and country.

Our  village has only got one street.

Cars can park on both sides of the street here.

villages, but not for country roads.

A  street is a road with houses on either side. We   use street for roads in towns or 1 the   difference 570 road      and   street and            ►   ɜ-ʒ rise        arise There are four chocolates for Penny, four for Joe and the rest are mine.

When  the rest refers to a plural noun, it has a plural verb.

If you divide 100 by 12, the remainder is 4.

Supper tonight is leftovers from lunch, (     .. . rests-.. .) not There were remains of the meal all over the floor, (     There-were rests-. ..) etc, we  often use other words.

To  talk about what is left after something has been used up, eaten, destroyed, We only use three rooms. The rest of the house is empty.

The rest means ‘ what is left'. It is singular in form, and the is always used.

Word  Problems from A  to Z     Section 31 vocabulary' •  572  say and tell not               youittomorrow.) ( I'll tell 'What time's the meeting? 'I'll tell you tomorrow.' We do not usually use it after tell to refer to a fact.

Alice said a naughty word this morning. (     Alice told...) Tell is not used before objects like a word, a name, a sentence, a phrase.

He's seven years old and he still can't tell the time.

difference, tell the time.

Note also the use of tell to mean ‘ distinguish’ , ‘ understand’ , as in tell the not I don't think she's telling the truth. (     ... saying the truth.) tell the truth, tell a lie, tell a story/joke.

Tell is used without a personal object in a few expressions. Common examples: not She told me that she would be late. (     She told that...) After tell, we  usually say who  is told.

And I say to all the people of this great country... (     And I say all not She said that she would be late. (     She said me that...) not He said a few words to me in Arabic. (     He said me a few words in Arabic.) not We   say something (to somebody),   say somebody something.

'What's your problem?' I said. (           Wh at's your problem?’ I told her.) but not Emily said, 'What a nice idea.' (         Emily told us, 'What a nice idea.') but not He said, 'Good morning.' (         He told them, 'Good morning’) greetings, exclamations or questions, for example.

Tell is only used to mean ‘ instruct’  or ‘ inform’ . So we  do not use tell with last chance.) or She said that it was my last chance. (   She told me that it was my or 'Turn right,' I said. (   'Turn right,' I told him.) than tell with direct speech.) Both say and tell are used with direct and indirect speech. (Say is more common 1 meaning       and   use 572 say    and   tell Joe and Kate went on a camping trip, and we're going to do the same.

Why do you always try to do the same as your brother?

Note also the expression do the same.

He's wearing the same shirt he had on yesterday.
